amoeba.description: Single-celled protozoans characterized by dynamic shape changes through cytoplasmic extensions called pseudopodia. Found in marine and freshwater environments.
amoeba.ecological_role: Primary consumer, feeds on bacteria and organic matter
amoeba.characteristics: Flexible cell membrane, pseudopodia for movement and feeding, no fixed shape

amphidinium_sp.description: Marine dinoflagellate with distinctive groove pattern. Important component of marine phytoplankton communities.

asterionellopsis.description: Chain-forming marine diatom with elongated, needle-like cells arranged in star-like colonies.
asterionellopsis.ecological_role: Primary producer, important component of marine food webs
asterionellopsis.characteristics: Colonial diatom, radiating chain formation, siliceous cell wall

cerataulina.description: Large chain-forming marine diatom with distinctive curved cells and prominent spines.
cerataulina.habitat: Coastal marine waters, often in high-nutrient areas
cerataulina.characteristics: Chain-forming, large cell size, curved morphology with spines

chaetoceros.description: Diverse genus of marine diatoms characterized by long, thin setae extending from cell corners. One of the most abundant diatom genera.
chaetoceros.habitat: Marine waters worldwide, particularly coastal areas
chaetoceros.ecological_role: Primary producer, major component of marine phytoplankton
chaetoceros.characteristics: Chain-forming, long setae, highly diverse morphology

chaetoceros_didymus.description: Specific species of Chaetoceros with characteristic paired cell arrangement and distinctive setae pattern.
chaetoceros_didymus.characteristics: Paired cells, specific setae arrangement, chain-forming

chaetoceros_other.description: Various species of Chaetoceros diatoms not specifically classified as C. didymus.

chrysochromulina.description: Golden-brown flagellated phytoplankton with two flagella of unequal length. Can form harmful algal blooms.
chrysochromulina.ecological_role: Primary producer, can cause harmful blooms
chrysochromulina.characteristics: Golden-brown coloration, two unequal flagella, small size

ciliate_mix.description: Diverse group of protozoa characterized by hair-like cilia used for movement and feeding.
ciliate_mix.characteristics: Cilia covering, complex cell structure, heterotrophic

corethron.description: Large marine diatom with cylindrical shape and distinctive polar spines.
corethron.characteristics: Cylindrical shape, prominent polar spines, large size

coscinodiscus.description: Large, disc-shaped marine diatom with radially arranged pores in cell wall.
coscinodiscus.characteristics: Disc-shaped, large size, radial pore pattern

cylindrotheca.description: Elongated pennate diatom with cylindrical shape, often found in coastal waters.

dactfragceraul.description: Fragmented or broken chains of Dactyliosolen or Cerataulina diatoms.

dactfragcerataul.description: Fragmented or broken chains of Dactyliosolen or Cerataulina diatoms.

dactyliosolen.description: Chain-forming marine diatom with finger-like projections, common in coastal waters.

delphineis.description: Pennate diatom with distinctive valve morphology, typically found attached to substrates.
delphineis.characteristics: Pennate symmetry, substrate attachment, distinctive valve pattern

dictyocha.description: Silicoflagellate with distinctive skeletal framework, important marine phytoplankter.

dinobryon.description: Colonial chrysophyte forming tree-like structures, common in freshwater but some marine species exist.

dinophysis.description: Marine dinoflagellate known for producing toxins causing diarrhetic shellfish poisoning.
dinophysis.characteristics: Compressed cell shape, toxin production, distinctive groove pattern

ditylum.description: Large marine diatom with distinctive horn-like projections, important in temperate waters.
ditylum.characteristics: Large size, horn-like projections, thick cell wall

ditylum_parasite.description: Ditylum diatoms infected with various parasites, often affecting cell morphology and health.
ditylum_parasite.characteristics: Modified morphology due to parasitic infection

emiliania_huxleyi.description: Coccolithophore with calcite plates, one of the most abundant phytoplankton species globally.
emiliania_huxleyi.ecological_role: Primary producer, major component of marine carbon cycle
emiliania_huxleyi.characteristics: Calcite coccoliths, spherical shape, globally distributed

ephemera.description: Temporary or short-lived planktonic organisms or structures.

eucampia.description: Chain-forming marine diatom with distinctive curved shape and linking structures.

euglena.description: Flagellated protist capable of both photosynthesis and heterotrophy, more common in freshwater.
euglena.characteristics: Single flagellum, flexible pellicle, mixotrophic nutrition

g_delicatula_external_parasite.description: Guinardia delicatula diatom with external parasitic organisms attached.
g_delicatula_external_parasite.characteristics: External parasitic attachments on diatom cells

g_delicatula_parasite.description: Guinardia delicatula diatom with internal parasitic infections.
g_delicatula_parasite.characteristics: Internal parasitic infections affecting cell structure

guinardia_delicatula.description: Chain-forming marine diatom, very common in coastal waters and important food source.
guinardia_delicatula.ecological_role: Primary producer, major food web component

guinardia_flaccida.description: Large chain-forming marine diatom with more flexible chain structure than G. delicatula.
guinardia_flaccida.characteristics: Large size, flexible chains, cylindrical cells

guinardia_striata.description: Marine diatom with distinctive striations on the cell wall.

gyrodinium.description: Unarmored dinoflagellate with distinctive spiral swimming pattern, some species form harmful blooms.
gyrodinium.ecological_role: Primary producer, some species cause harmful blooms

heterocapsa_triquetra.description: Small marine dinoflagellate with triangular shape when viewed from apex.
heterocapsa_triquetra.characteristics: Triangular apical view, small size, armored dinoflagellate

katodinium_or_torodinium.description: Small heterotrophic dinoflagellates, important microbial grazers in marine ecosystems.
katodinium_or_torodinium.characteristics: Small size, heterotrophic, distinctive dinoflagellate morphology

laboea_strobila.description: Mixotrophic ciliate that retains chloroplasts from prey, combining grazing and photosynthesis.
laboea_strobila.characteristics: Ciliate with retained chloroplasts, mixotrophic nutrition

leptocylindrus.description: Thin, elongated chain-forming marine diatom, often very abundant in coastal waters.
leptocylindrus.characteristics: Very thin cylindrical shape, chain-forming, high length to width ratio

leptocylindrus_mediterraneus.description: Species of Leptocylindrus originally described from Mediterranean Sea.
leptocylindrus_mediterraneus.characteristics: Thin cylindrical morphology, specific to Mediterranean region

licmophora.description: Fan-shaped colonial pennate diatom, typically attached to substrates.
licmophora.characteristics: Fan-shaped colonies, pennate diatom, substrate attachment

mesodinium_sp.description: Ciliate that can retain chloroplasts from cryptophyte prey, forming red tide blooms.
mesodinium_sp.characteristics: Ciliate with acquired photosynthesis, can cause red coloration

paralia.description: Drum-shaped marine diatom often found in chains, common in coastal areas.

parvicorbicula_socialis.description: Small colonial marine diatom forming social aggregations.
parvicorbicula_socialis.characteristics: Small size, colonial behavior, social aggregations

phaeocystis.description: Colonial haptophyte forming large mucilaginous colonies, important in polar and temperate waters.
phaeocystis.characteristics: Colonial mucilaginous structure, flagellated cells, bloom-forming

pleuronema_sp.description: Free-swimming ciliate with distinctive undulating membrane, heterotrophic grazer.

pleurosigma.description: S-shaped pennate diatom with distinctive sigmoid morphology.

prorocentrum.description: Heart-shaped dinoflagellate, some species produce toxins and form harmful blooms.
prorocentrum.ecological_role: Primary producer, some species form toxic blooms
prorocentrum.characteristics: Heart-shaped, two-plate dinoflagellate, some toxic species

proterythropsis_sp.description: Small flagellated protist, part of the microbial food web.

pseudochattonella_farcimen.description: Raphidophyte flagellate capable of forming harmful algal blooms.

pseudonitzschia.description: Needle-shaped pennate diatom, some species produce domoic acid toxin.
pseudonitzschia.ecological_role: Primary producer, some species produce neurotoxins
pseudonitzschia.characteristics: Needle-shaped, stepped chains, some toxic species

pyramimonas_longicauda.description: Quadriflagellate green algae with distinctive long tail-like projection.
pyramimonas_longicauda.characteristics: Four flagella, pyramid-shaped, long tail projection

rhizosolenia.description: Large, needle-shaped marine diatom, important component of oceanic phytoplankton.

skeletonema.description: Small chain-forming marine diatom connected by linking threads, very abundant cosmopolitan species.
skeletonema.characteristics: Small cylindrical cells, linking threads, chain-forming

strobilidium_morphotype1.description: Oligotrich ciliate with distinctive morphology, important marine micrograzer.

strombidium_morphotype1.description: Oligotrich ciliate, important component of marine microbial food webs.
strombidium_morphotype1.characteristics: Conical shape, prominent oral cilia, heterotrophic

strombidium_morphotype2.description: Alternative morphological form of Strombidium ciliate.

strombidium_oculatum.description: Specific species of Strombidium ciliate with eye-like features.

thalassionema.description: Needle-shaped pennate diatom often found in zig-zag chains.
thalassionema.characteristics: Needle-shaped, zig-zag chain formation, pennate diatom

thalassiosira.description: Centric diatom with radial symmetry, one of the most diverse marine diatom genera.
thalassiosira.characteristics: Centric symmetry, diverse morphologies, marine distribution

thalassiosira_dirty.description: Thalassiosira diatoms with attached organic or inorganic debris particles.

tintinnid.description: Loricate ciliates with distinctive cone-shaped shells, important marine microzooplankton.

tontonia_gracillima.description: Oligotrich ciliate with gracile morphology, marine micrograzer.

bead.description: Artificial spherical particles used for calibration or contamination in samples.

diatom_flagellate.description: Mixed assemblage or association of diatoms and flagellates in same field of view.
diatom_flagellate.characteristics: Combination of diatom and flagellate morphologies

dino30.description: Unidentified dinoflagellate species approximately 30 micrometers in size.

other_interaction.description: Images showing interactions between different plankton species or with particles.

pennate.description: General category of pennate (bilaterally symmetric) diatoms not further classified.
pennate.characteristics: Bilateral symmetry, elongated shape, pennate structure

pennate_morphotype1.description: Specific morphological type of pennate diatom with distinctive features.

pennates_on_diatoms.description: Small pennate diatoms growing attached to larger diatom cells.
pennates_on_diatoms.characteristics: Small size, attached to host diatoms, epiphytic lifestyle

spore.description: Reproductive spores from various marine organisms, including algae and fungi.
spore.characteristics: Spherical to oval, thick-walled, resistant structures
